title: Plus500 Pricing Explained: All Plans, Costs & Fees (2026)

excerpt: Complete breakdown of Plus500 pricing — all plans, hidden fees, and how to save money.
content:
Plus500 Pricing Overview

The Bottom Line: Plus500 offers completely free account registration and maintenance across all account types—demo, live, and professional accounts all cost $0/month with no hidden subscription fees. However, this doesn't mean trading is free. Costs come through spreads (the difference between bid and ask prices), overnight holding fees for positions held beyond market close, and other trading-related charges.

For traders looking to get started in CFD and forex trading without upfront platform costs, Plus500 is genuinely free to begin. But understanding the real cost structure is essential before depositing real money.

All Plus500 Plans Compared

Account Type Monthly Cost Key Features Best For
Demo Account $0 Unlimited virtual funds, all market access, real-time quotes, full platform features, paper trading Learning, strategy testing, risk-free practice
Live Account $0 Real money trading, commission-free execution, spreads apply, all markets, risk management tools Active traders, beginners with small deposits
Professional Account $0 All live features plus higher leverage (up to 30:1 vs 1:30 for retail), tighter spreads, dedicated support Experienced traders, institutional clients, high-volume traders

Account Upgrade Path: You can start with a demo account indefinitely at no cost. When ready to trade live, you simply open a live account with a minimum deposit (typically $100–$500, depending on your region). The professional account requires meeting specific criteria (typically $500,000+ trading volume or account size, plus demonstrated trading experience).

Free Plan / Free Trial

Plus500's demo account is truly free and unlimited. There is no trial period that expires—you can practice trading with virtual money indefinitely without any time restrictions or hidden activation of a paid tier.

  • Demo Account Features: You get $100,000 in virtual funds to trade live prices across forex, stocks, indices, commodities, and cryptocurrencies. The experience is identical to the live platform, so you're testing your strategies with genuine order execution and market conditions.
  • Demo Limitations: The virtual funds reset if you request a new account, and the psychological element of real money is absent—emotions and position sizing often differ when real capital is at risk.
  • Worth It? Absolutely. The demo is one of Plus500's strongest features. Unlike many brokers that limit demo access to 30 days, Plus500's unlimited demo is excellent for beginners and for testing new strategies without risking capital.

Hidden Costs and Fees

While Plus500 charges $0 for account maintenance, real costs emerge when you trade. Here's what to expect:

  • Spreads (Primary Cost): On major forex pairs (EURUSD, GBPUSD), spreads typically start at 1-2 pips. On stocks and indices, spreads are wider—often 2-5% of the bid-ask width. This is your primary trading cost. There are no commissions, but spreads replace them. A $1,000 forex trade with a 1.5 pip spread costs ~$15.
  • Overnight Financing (Holding Charges): If you hold a CFD position overnight, Plus500 charges financing costs based on the notional value, leverage used, and your position direction. Long positions typically cost slightly more than short positions. These can accumulate significantly for swing traders or position traders.
  • Inactivity Fee: This is a critical hidden cost. If your account is inactive for 3 consecutive months, Plus500 charges a monthly inactivity fee of €10 (~$11 USD) or equivalent in your currency. This fee continues until the account is closed or activity resumes. Many users discover this when revisiting dormant accounts.
  • Withdrawal Fees: In some regions, Plus500 charges withdrawal fees (typically €5–€10 depending on your country and withdrawal method). This varies by jurisdiction and payment processor.
  • Slippage: During volatile market conditions, your execution price may differ from the quoted price. Plus500's execution is generally reliable, but slippage is possible on illiquid instruments.
  • Guaranteed Stop Loss Orders: This premium feature (offered to manage tail risk) carries a small fee—typically a few cents per trade depending on instrument and position size.
  • Data Feeds: Real-time data is included for free. Delayed data is also available at no cost, so there are no hidden data subscription fees.

Example Real Cost: A trader depositing $1,000 and making 10 daily round-trip trades on EURUSD with 2:1 leverage faces roughly $150–$200 in spread costs per month (assuming 1.5 pip average spread), plus potential overnight financing charges of $5–$15 depending on position duration. Annual trading costs could reach $1,800–$2,400 with active trading.

Plus500 Pricing vs Competitors

Let's compare Plus500's cost structure to three direct competitors in the CFD and forex space:

Broker Account Fee EURUSD Spread Commission Overnight Fee
Plus500 $0 1–2 pips $0 Yes (varies)
IG (CFD) $0 0.6–1.5 pips $0 Yes (similar)
CMC Markets $0 0.9–1.5 pips $0 Yes (similar)
Interactive Brokers $0 (but $10/mo min commission) 0.1–0.3 pips $0.01–$0.02/share or % per lot Yes (variable)

Value Assessment:

  • Plus500 vs IG: Both offer excellent platforms and are FCA-regulated. IG tends to have slightly tighter spreads (0.6–1.5 vs 1–2 pips), making it better for high-frequency traders. Plus500 is simpler and better for beginners. Pricing difference is marginal.
  • Plus500 vs CMC Markets: Very similar offerings. CMC Markets has slightly better charting tools and API access. Cost difference is negligible. CMC Markets edges Plus500 for professional traders; Plus500 edges CMC for mobile-first traders.
  • Plus500 vs Interactive Brokers: Interactive Brokers has much tighter spreads (0.1–0.3 pips) but charges commissions ($10/month minimum). For active traders making 50+ trades per month, Interactive Brokers wins on total cost. For casual traders, Plus500's spread-only model is cheaper. Interactive Brokers requires $10,000 minimum deposit; Plus500 typically $100–$500.

Winner by Use Case:

  • Cost-conscious beginners: Plus500 (lowest entry barrier, unlimited demo)
  • High-frequency traders: Interactive Brokers (tighter spreads despite commissions)
  • Professional charting: IG (superior tools, competitive spreads)

Is Plus500 Worth the Price?

Short Answer: Yes, if you're a beginner or casual CFD trader. No, if you're a high-volume trader who could benefit from lower commissions.

Best For:

  • Beginner traders: The $0 account cost and unlimited demo make this ideal for learning. The simple proprietary platform is less intimidating than MetaTrader 4/5. No minimum deposit barrier for paper trading.
  • Mobile-first traders: Plus500's mobile app is consistently rated 4.5+/5 stars. If you trade primarily on your phone, this is one of the best-in-class experiences.
  • Short-term forex traders: For scalpers and day traders focused on major pairs, Plus500's 1–2 pip spreads are competitive. The inactivity fee won't affect you if you trade regularly.
  • Casual CFD traders: Trading stocks, indices, or commodities occasionally? The flat spread model means costs are proportional to your trading volume, with no surprise account fees.

NOT Best For:

  • Algorithmic traders: No API or automated trading support. Plus500 restricts algo trading and has no way to plug in external strategies.
  • Charting enthusiasts: Limited technical analysis tools and no support for third-party platforms like TradingView or MetaTrader 4/5. Serious chartists will feel constrained.
  • High-volume traders: If you're making 100+ trades per month, the cumulative spread costs ($50–$200+/month) likely exceed what you'd pay on a commission-based platform with tighter spreads.
  • Long-term holders: If you hold CFD positions for weeks or months, overnight financing fees compound. This model is built for shorter-term trading.

When to Upgrade to Professional Account: If you've proven consistent profitability and have capital to deploy, a professional account's higher leverage (30:1 vs 1:30 retail) and tighter spreads justify the effort to qualify. However, Plus500 doesn't charge extra for this—it's a regulatory classification, not a paid upgrade.

How to Save on Plus500

Since Plus500 charges no subscription fees and offers no tiered pricing, cost reduction focuses on trading efficiency:

  • Use the Demo First: This is free and unlimited. Spend weeks or months perfecting your strategy on the demo before risking real capital. A trader who reduces losing trades by 10% saves far more than any fee discount could offer.
  • Avoid the Inactivity Fee: Even if you're not actively trading, log in or place one trade every 3 months to reset the inactivity counter. This $10/month fee is entirely avoidable with basic account management.
  • Trade Major Pairs Only: The tightest spreads are on EURUSD, GBPUSD, and USDJPY. Exotic pairs like NZDUSD or USDSGD have 3–5 pip spreads. Stick to major pairs if cost-conscious.
  • Minimize Overnight Holds: Overnight financing fees add up. If possible, close positions before the market close. For swing traders, this cost is unavoidable, but day traders can sidestep it entirely.
  • No Discount Codes: Plus500 doesn't offer promotional discount codes or seasonal pricing. Occasionally, they run sign-up bonuses (typically $30–$100 credit), but these are rare and region-specific. Check their promotions page during account opening.
  • Tax Efficiency (Advanced): Depending on your jurisdiction, CFD trading may have different tax treatment than stock trading. Consult a tax professional—this can sometimes reduce costs more than any broker fee negotiation.

The Hard Truth: There's no magic discount on Plus500. The real savings come from trading better—fewer losing trades, smaller position sizes, and less time holding overnight positions. A $100/month reduction in unnecessary trades saves more than hunting for a 0.2 pip spread reduction.
